proofs.name: Addition Commutativity
proofs.description: For all natural numbers a and b, a + b = b + a
proofs.lean_code:
theorem add_comm_proof (a b : Nat) : a + b = b + a := by
  induction a with
  | zero => simp
  | succ a' ih =>
    rw [Nat.add_succ, ih, Nat.add_succ]

proofs.name: Distributivity of Multiplication over Addition
proofs.description: a * (b + c) = a * b + a * c
proofs.lean_code:
theorem distrib_proof (a b c : Nat) : a * (b + c) = a * b + a * c := by
  induction a with
  | zero => simp
  | succ a' ih =>
    rw [Nat.mul_succ, ih, Nat.mul_succ, Nat.add_assoc]

proofs.name: Sum of Even Numbers is Even
proofs.description: If a and b are even, then a + b is even
proofs.lean_code:
theorem even_add_even {a b : Nat} (ha : Even a) (hb : Even b) : Even (a + b) := by
  rw [even_iff_two_dvd] at ha hb ⊢
  obtain ⟨k, rfl⟩ := ha
  obtain ⟨l, rfl⟩ := hb
  use k + l
  ring

proofs.name: Product of Odd Numbers is Odd
proofs.description: If a and b are odd, then a * b is odd
proofs.lean_code:
theorem odd_mul_odd {a b : Nat} (ha : Odd a) (hb : Odd b) : Odd (a * b) := by
  cases' ha with k hk
  cases' hb with l hl
  rw [hk, hl]
  use 2 * k * l + k + l
  ring

proofs.name: De Morgan's Law: ¬(P ∧ Q) ↔ ¬P ∨ ¬Q
proofs.description: Negation of conjunction equals disjunction of negations
proofs.lean_code:
theorem de_morgan_and (P Q : Prop) : ¬(P ∧ Q) ↔ ¬P ∨ ¬Q := by
  apply Iff.intro
  · intro h
    by_cases
    · assume hP : P
      have hQ : Q := by
        by_contra h
        exact h ⟨hP, h⟩
      contradiction
    · exact Or.inr ‹¬Q›
  · intro h
    cases h
    · intro hPQ
      exact ‹¬P› hPQ.left
    · intro hPQ
      exact ‹¬Q› hPQ.right

proofs.name: Sum Formula: 2 × Σ(i=0..n) i = n × (n+1)
proofs.description: The sum of first n natural numbers equals n(n+1)/2
proofs.lean_code:
theorem sum_first_n_formula (n : Nat) : 
    2 * (∑ i in Finset.range (n + 1), i) = n * (n + 1) := by
  induction n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n ih =>
    rw [Finset.sum_range_succ, ih]
    ring

proofs.name: Geometric Series Sum: Σ(i=0..n) 2^i = 2^(n+1) - 1
proofs.description: Sum of powers of 2 equals next power minus 1
proofs.lean_code:
theorem geometric_series_sum (n : Nat) : 
    (∑ i in Finset.range (n + 1), 2^i) = 2^(n + 1) - 1 := by
  induction n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n ih =>
    rw [Finset.sum_range_succ, ih]
    have : 2^(n + 1) + 2^(n + 1) = 2^(n + 2) := by
      rw [← Nat.mul_two, ← pow_succ]
    rw [this]
    simp

proofs.name: Divisibility Transitivity
proofs.description: If a|b and b|c, then a|c
proofs.lean_code:
theorem dvd_trans {a b c : Nat} (hab : a ∣ b) (hbc : b ∣ c) : a ∣ c := by
  obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := hab
  obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ := hbc
  use k * l
  rw [hl, hk, mul_assoc]

proofs.name: √2 is Irrational
proofs.description: The square root of 2 cannot be expressed as a ratio
proofs.lean_code:
theorem sqrt_two_irrational' : ¬∃ (p q : Nat), q ≠ 0 ∧ p * p = 2 * q * q := by
  intro h
  obtain ⟨p, q, hq, hpq⟩ := h
  -- Classical proof by infinite descent
  have : p % 2 = 0 := by
    have : 2 ∣ p * p := by
      use q * q
      linarith
    exact Nat.Prime.dvd_of_dvd_pow Nat.prime_two this
  obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := this
  rw [hk] at hpq
  ring_nf at hpq
  have : q % 2 = 0 := by
    have : 2 ∣ q * q := by
      use k * k
      linarith
    exact Nat.Prime.dvd_of_dvd_pow Nat.prime_two this
  sorry  -- Full proof requires descent argument

proofs.name: Infinitude of Primes
proofs.description: There are infinitely many prime numbers
proofs.lean_code:
theorem infinitude_of_primes : ∀ n : Nat, ∃ p > n, Nat.Prime p := by
  intro n
  have : ∃ p, Nat.Prime p ∧ p ∣ (n + 1).factorial + 1 := by
    apply Nat.exists_prime_and_dvd
    simp
  obtain ⟨p, hp, hpdvd⟩ := this
  use p
  constructor
  · show p > n
    by_contra h
    push_neg at h
    have : p ∣ (n + 1).factorial := by
      apply Nat.dvd_factorial
      · exact hp.pos
      · linarith
    have : p ∣ 1 := by
      apply Nat.dvd_sub' hpdvd this
    simp at this
    contradiction
  · exact hp

proofs.name: Bezout's Identity
proofs.description: For any a,b there exist x,y such that ax + by = gcd(a,b)
proofs.lean_code:
theorem bezout_identity (a b : Nat) : 
    ∃ x y : Int, a * x + b * y = Nat.gcd a b := by
  use Nat.gcdA a b, Nat.gcdB a b
  apply Nat.gcd_eq_gcd_ab

proofs.name: Fermat's Little Theorem
proofs.description: If p is prime and p ∤ a, then a^(p-1) ≡ 1 (mod p)
proofs.lean_code:
theorem fermat_little {a p : Nat} (hp : Nat.Prime p) (h : ¬p ∣ a) : 
    a^(p - 1) ≡ 1 [MOD p] := by
  apply Nat.ModEq.pow_card_sub_one'
  intro h
  apply h
  exact Nat.Prime.dvd_of_dvd_pow hp h

proofs.name: Pigeonhole Principle
proofs.description: If n+1 pigeons in n holes, some hole has ≥2 pigeons
proofs.lean_code:
theorem pigeonhole_simple {α β : Type} [Fintype α] [Fintype β] 
    (f : α → β) (h : Fintype.card α > Fintype.card β) : 
    ∃ (b : β), ∃ (a₁ a₂ : α), a₁ ≠ a₂ ∧ f a₁ = b ∧ f a₂ = b := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  have : Function.Injective f := by
    intro a₁ a₂ hf
    by_contra hne
    specialize h' (f a₁)
    simp at h'
    contradiction
  have : Fintype.card α ≤ Fintype.card β := by
    apply Fintype.card_le_of_injective f this
  linarith

proofs.name: Binomial Expansion (a+b)²
proofs.lean_code:
theorem binomial_square (a b : Nat) : (a + b)^2 = a^2 + 2*a*b + b^2 := by
  rw [pow_two, pow_two, pow_two]
  ring

proofs.name: Power Set Has Greater Cardinality
proofs.lean_code:
theorem cantor_theorem {α : Type} : 
    ¬∃ (f : α → Set α), Function.Bijective f := by
  intro h
  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
  let S := {x : α | x ∉ f x}
  obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := hf.surjective S
  have h₁ : s ∉ f s := by
    intro h'
    have : s ∉ f s := hs s
    contradiction
  have h₂ : s ∈ S := by
    have : f s = S := hs
    rw [← this]
    exact h₁
  contradiction
